mapping.a_element: classical molecular dynamics simulation of charged particles in a magnetic field
mapping.b_element: analytical/numerical study of charged billiard ball dynamics in a magnetic field

mapping.a_element: rectangular billiard geometry with external magnetic field
mapping.b_element: billiard in plane domains (e.g., square) with perpendicular magnetic field

mapping.a_element: regularity islands at integer aspect ratios (single-particle case)
mapping.b_element: invariant tori corresponding to skipping trajectories in convex domains

mapping.a_element: chaotic behavior in two-particle Coulomb-interacting system
mapping.b_element: ergodicity and chaotic scattering in multi-obstacle billiards (e.g., two circles)

mapping.a_element: transition from quasiperiodicity to chaos with varying interaction strength
mapping.b_element: bifurcation and stability analysis of periodic orbits via Jacobian matrix of the bouncing map

mapping.a_element: strong magnetic field leading to regular trajectories
mapping.b_element: adiabatic invariants in strong magnetic fields over exponentially long times

brief_justification: Both papers study the dynamics of charged particles in a magnetic field within a billiard framework, sharing the core mechanism of Hamiltonian chaos and integrability in magnetic fields. The molecular dynamics approach in A maps to the bouncing map and stability analysis in B, with regular/chaotic transitions and magnetic field effects playing analogous roles in both.
